---
title: "Exploratory Data Analysis with Python"
description: "A walkthrough of a simple EDA workflow using pandas and matplotlib."
author: "Jonathan Pearce"
date: "2026-02-15"
categories: [Python, EDA, Tutorial]
image: thumbnail.png
toc: true
code-fold: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

## Overview

This example post demonstrates how a typical Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) blog post looks on *Casual Inference*. It generates synthetic data so the post renders without external dependencies.

## Generate Data

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

np.random.seed(123)

n = 300
df = pd.DataFrame({
    "age": np.random.randint(18, 65, n),
    "income": np.random.exponential(50000, n).round(2),
    "score": np.random.beta(2, 5, n).round(4),
})
df.head()
```

## Distributions

```{python}
#| label: fig-distributions
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of age, income, and score."

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(12, 3.5))
for ax, col in zip(axes, df.columns):
    ax.hist(df[col], bins=30, edgecolor="white")
    ax.set_title(col.capitalize())
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Correlations

```{python}
df.corr().round(3)
```

## Next Steps

- Handle outliers
- Feature engineering
- Modelling
